pundit.name: Eric Wynalda
pundit.take: **Gunner is the only one here who remotely understands what a competent draft looks like.** Everyone else, frankly, is just embarrassing themselves, and it's fascinating to watch. "Mustard Boy" Zach, you picked Saudi Arabia with a straight face? I guess if you're trying to build a retirement home for World Cup flops, that makes sense. Your total of 23 points isn't surprising when you draft like a FIFA video gamer who just recognizes flags. Devin's "Ghost Pepper" persona might be hot, but his draft certainly isn't—Spain and the USA couldn't even stop Gunner's France-Senegal onslaught. And "The Noisemaker" Rafe, you might as well have thrown darts blindfolded with three non-qualifiers. The standings don't lie: Gunner's sitting pretty at 28 points for a reason, and it's not luck—it's because he knew what he was doing. Those other managers should take notes and maybe, just maybe, they can pretend to know soccer next time around.

pundit_takes.headline: Gunner's Tactical Genius Evident as France Crushes Norway 4-1
pundit_takes.subtitle: Sources say Devin considering switching to knitting after humiliating defeat.

pundit_takes.headline: Gayden's England Set to Dominate Undrafted Panama in New Jersey
pundit_takes.subtitle: 'It's nice when the opponents don't have a manager,' Gayden admits.

pundit_takes.headline: Rafe's Germany Still Stuck in Neutral Gear Despite Top Tier Pick
pundit_takes.subtitle: Third in standings with teams still trying to find the goalpost.

pundit_takes.headline: Mustard Boy's Austrian Underdogs Poised for Glory Against Algeria
pundit_takes.subtitle: If there’s one thing Zach knows, it's how to spice up a match.

rome_analytics_quotes: Gunner goes from 4th to 1st—and chances are, he's loving every minute.
Rafe's Tier-1 knowledge is as sharp as a balloon at a dart show.
Devin's 0-6 against Rafe—sounds like the Ghost Pepper's lost its heat.
Model predicts chaos; Rafe delivers—15 and unpredictable, just like his draft strategy.
